title: 10 Generating Reports
body_text:
10	Generating Reports
10.1	Overview of Generating Reports
The Manage Reports function allow User Management Security Officers (Security Officers) to generate reports. Reports are in a CSV format. Depending on the report type requested, users download the CSV file from User Management or receive it via a secure email.
The following reports are available:
Profile Report
ГўВҖВ“ Contains a list of users, their user type, last login date, and the date that each user last changed their password.
PaymentStream
AFT Permissions Report
ГўВҖВ“ Contains a list of
PaymentStream
AFT users and the usersГўВҖВҷ permissions.
List of Business for Organization
ГўВҖВ“ Contains
a list of businesses, their addresses and contact information.
List Applications for Organization
ГўВҖВ“ Contains a list of users and their assigned applications.
Note:	The List Applications for Organization report is sent to the Security Officer generating the report via secure email. It isnГўВҖВҷt downloaded from the User Management application. See Section 10.2, Generating Reports, for more information.
10.2	Generating Reports
Generate a report as follows. See Figure 1.
Select
Manage Reports
, then select a report type. Complete the Business field to request a report by business if appropriate.
Select
Generate Report
.
Figure 1: Generating a Report
Follow the prompts to access the report. If youГўВҖВҷre generating a report thatГўВҖВҷs sent by secure email, a message displays advising you that the report will be sent to your email address. See Figure 2. See Section 10.3, Accessing Reports Sent by Secure Email, for procedures.
Figure 2: Generating a Report
10.3	Accessing Reports Sent by Secure Email
Certain reports generated in User Management are sent to the Security Officer via Cisco
ГӮВ®
Registered Envelope Service (CRES). See Section 10.1, Overview of Generating Reports, for more information. Registration is required to obtain the keys necessary to view the contents of the secure email. Users only need to register once.
Access a report received via secure email as follows:
Open the secure email, then double-click on the attached file. See Figure 3. Select
Open
when prompted. Alternatively, select
Save
to save the attachment to your computer.
Figure 3: Download Message Attachment
If you have previously registered with CRES:
Enter your password, then select
Open Online
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Logging in to CRES
Select the CSV link to download the report. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Downloading the CSV Report
Note:	Secure emails are accessible for one year.
If you donГўВҖВҷt have a CRES account, youГўВҖВҷre prompted to register for one:
Select
Register
on the Cisco Secure Message Service. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Select ГўВҖВңRegisterГўВҖВқ
Complete the registration fields, agree to the terms of service, then select
Register
. See FigureВ 7.
Figure 7: Complete Registration
A message displays confirming your account is created. You must activate your account to read your message. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Account Successfully Created
Open the email from ГўВҖВңDoNotReply@res.cisco.comГўВҖВқ then select
Activate Account
. See Figure 9. A confirmation message displays when your activation is complete.
Important
:	Your account is unique to your email address, donГўВҖВҷt share your password.
Figure 9: Activate Account
Go to your message, open the attachment and log in to CRES to see your message as previously described.
END
